flags.message: This looks like a pre-1925 mandatory-jurisdiction SCOTUS case (consolidated docket numbers '522, 523, 524', an attached opinion cluster, no cert dates) — the population the predict-scope exclusions target — yet it reached the predict matrix, likely because the multi-number docket string defeats the Term/docket parser. 'Petition disposition' is a poor fit for an era with no discretionary cert gate; I predicted a merits disposition ('other') calibrated on corpus base rates. Consider whether the scope reconcile should latch this case out.

The snapshot is nearly bare: no docket entries, no parties, no panel, and every date field null. The only substantive signals are the docket-number string and the linked opinion cluster. If this case stays in scope, a recoverability probe of the linked cluster would materially improve the snapshot.
